issues.title: Coverage: Configuration: organization units (libraries, departments, circ desks)

tests.scope: Exercise every read-only org-unit method shipped in issue #24 against live SANDBOX. Calls list_libraries() and list_departments() to confirm both return List[Dict] envelopes successfully unwrapped from Alma's response shape; calls get_library(${existing_library_code}) to confirm a known library code round-trips and the returned dict echoes the requested code; calls list_circ_desks(${target_library_code}) to confirm the per-library circ-desk listing returns a List[Dict]; and calls get_circ_desk(${target_library_code}, ${existing_circ_desk_code}) to confirm the per-library/per-desk lookup round-trips. Two library fixture keys: existing_library_code is used for the get_library smoke (operator may scope reads to a different library than the one used for circ-desk / locations work); target_library_code is the library that hosts the circ desk and (later) the round-trip create/update/delete in t-25-1. No state is mutated and no new entities are created — this is a pure read smoke. Covers issue #24 ACs about each method being exercised against SANDBOX and the AlmaAPIError happy-path.

needsHumanInput.description: An existing Alma library code in SANDBOX. Used ONLY for the get_library() read smoke. May be the same as target_library_code, or a different library if the operator wants reads scoped to one library and circ-desk / location work scoped to another. Synthetic placeholder only — the SANDBOX-test runner substitutes a real value at runtime; never write a real library code into this file.

An existing Alma library code in SANDBOX that hosts the circ desk under existing_circ_desk_code AND where the operator authorizes test locations to be created and deleted. Used by list_circ_desks / get_circ_desk in this test, and by the round-trip create/update/delete in t-25-1. Synthetic placeholder only — never write a real library code into this file.

An existing circulation-desk code under target_library_code in SANDBOX. Used by get_circ_desk to confirm the per-library/per-desk lookup round-trips. Synthetic placeholder only — never write a real circ-desk code into this file.

pythonCalls: from almaapitk.domains.configuration import Configuration
config = Configuration(client)
libraries = config.list_libraries()
departments = config.list_departments()
library = config.get_library('${existing_library_code}')
circ_desks = config.list_circ_desks('${target_library_code}')
circ_desk = config.get_circ_desk('${target_library_code}', '${existing_circ_desk_code}')
passCriteria: (library.get('code') == '${existing_library_code}') or (isinstance(library.get('code'), dict) and library.get('code', {}).get('value') == '${existing_library_code}') or any(str(v) == '${existing_library_code}' for v in library.values() if isinstance(v, str))

(circ_desk.get('code') == '${existing_circ_desk_code}') or (isinstance(circ_desk.get('code'), dict) and circ_desk.get('code', {}).get('value') == '${existing_circ_desk_code}') or any(str(v) == '${existing_circ_desk_code}' for v in circ_desk.values() if isinstance(v, str))

tests.scope: Confirm every input-validation guard added in issue #24 raises AlmaValidationError BEFORE any HTTP call is issued. Exercises Configuration._validate_code through every public entry point: get_library('') / get_library(None) / get_library(123); list_circ_desks('') / list_circ_desks(None); get_circ_desk('', 'x') / get_circ_desk('LIB', '') / get_circ_desk(None, None). list_libraries() and list_departments() take no arguments so have no validation surface — they are not exercised here. Pure runtime guard exercise; no SANDBOX state is read or mutated. Covers the AlmaValidationError half of issue #24 AC 'Errors raise AlmaValidationError (input) or AlmaAPIError / subclass (API).'
pythonCalls: from almaapitk.domains.configuration import Configuration
from almaapitk.client.AlmaAPIClient import AlmaValidationError
config = Configuration(client)
errors = {}

unmappable.ac: All listed methods implemented on the existing domain class with type hints and docstrings.
unmappable.reason: The 'with type hints and docstrings' facet is a static-source invariant that cannot be observed via SANDBOX behaviour — it is verified by code review of the chunk diff and (transitively) by tests/test_public_api_contract.py. The 'methods exist on the existing Configuration class' facet IS observable at runtime (t-24-1 only succeeds if list_libraries/get_library/list_departments/list_circ_desks/get_circ_desk are all importable and bound on Configuration), so the AC is partially mapped to t-24-1; this entry documents the residual static-only facet.

unmappable.ac: Each method has unit-test coverage (mocked HTTP) and at least one integration test against SANDBOX.
unmappable.reason: The 'unit-test coverage (mocked HTTP)' facet is a local pytest-suite deliverable (tests/unit/domains/test_configuration.py) verified pre-merge by the test runner, not by Alma SANDBOX behaviour. The 'at least one integration test against SANDBOX' facet IS exercised by t-24-1 (every new method touches live SANDBOX); this entry documents the unit-test residual.

The input-validation half (AlmaValidationError on bad inputs) is mapped to t-24-2. The API-error half (AlmaAPIError/subclass on real Alma failures) is harder to provoke deterministically against SANDBOX — most failure paths (404 on a non-existent library/circ desk, 401 on bad auth) are environment-dependent and would flake. Existing unit tests in tests/unit/domains/test_configuration.py cover the API-error path with mocked HTTP; this entry documents that residual unit-only facet.

Logging-call invariant on the source. SANDBOX returns identical bytes regardless of whether the wrapper logs or prints; observable only by inspecting the source (no `print(` tokens in the new methods) and by reviewing the `logs/` directory after the chunk runs. Verified by code review of the chunk diff.

Local-suite invariant. Both are run by the operator (and CI) in the local environment; no Alma SANDBOX call would observe their outcome. Verified pre-merge by `poetry run python scripts/smoke_import.py` and `poetry run pytest tests/test_public_api_contract.py -v`, not by SANDBOX behaviour.

unmappable.ac: If new public symbols are exposed, they are added to `almaapitk/__init__.py` `__all__` and the lazy import map.
unmappable.reason: Module-structure invariant on src/almaapitk/__init__.py. Issue #24 extends the existing Configuration class (already exported via the issue #22 bootstrap) — no NEW public symbols are introduced, so this AC is vacuously satisfied. Verified by inspecting the chunk diff against src/almaapitk/__init__.py (no changes expected) and by the existing public-API contract test, neither of which is a SANDBOX observation.

tests.scope: Drive the full locations CRUD round-trip end-to-end against live SANDBOX. Generates a fresh location code with a UUID suffix (so concurrent runs do not collide), creates a new location inside ${target_library_code} via create_location() with a minimal valid payload (code/name/type), reads it back via get_location() and asserts the round-trip; lists via list_locations() and asserts the new code is present; mutates the name and pushes via update_location(), then reads again and asserts the rename landed; finally deletes via delete_location() and asserts a follow-up get_location() raises AlmaAPIError (the 4xx for 'no longer exists'). Cleanup runs delete_location in a try/finally so a partially-created location is always torn down even on assertion failure. target_library_code is the same key supplied for t-24-1 — the library where the operator has authorized test mutations. Covers issue #25 ACs about each CRUD method being exercised against SANDBOX, validation guards firing for required fields (because a successful create only happens if the validator accepts the payload), and the round-trip flow itself.

needsHumanInput.description: An existing Alma library code in SANDBOX where the operator authorizes test locations to be created and deleted. Same key supplied for t-24-1. Synthetic placeholder only — the SANDBOX-test runner substitutes a real value at runtime; never write a real library code into this file.

pythonCalls: import uuid
from almaapitk.domains.configuration import Configuration
from almaapitk.client.AlmaAPIClient import AlmaAPIError
config = Configuration(client)
new_location_code = f'TKLOC{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8].upper()}'

create_payload = {
    'code': new_location_code,
    'name': f'almaapitk chunk-25 round-trip location {new_location_code}',
    'type': {'value': 'OPEN'},
    'external_name': f'almaapitk chunk-25 location {new_location_code}',
    'description': 'almaapitk chunk-25 round-trip location (auto-cleanup)',
}
create_response = config.create_location('${target_library_code}', create_payload)
post_create_get = config.get_location('${target_library_code}', new_location_code)
post_create_listing = config.list_locations('${target_library_code}')

update_payload = dict(post_create_get) if isinstance(post_create_get, dict) else dict(create_payload)
update_payload['name'] = f'almaapitk chunk-25 location {new_location_code} (renamed)'
update_response = config.update_location('${target_library_code}', new_location_code, update_payload)
post_update_get = config.get_location('${target_library_code}', new_location_code)
post_update_name = post_update_get.get('name') if isinstance(post_update_get, dict) else None
delete_response = config.delete_location('${target_library_code}', new_location_code)
passCriteria: create_response is not None and getattr(create_response, 'success', False) is True
(post_create_get.get('code') == new_location_code) or (isinstance(post_create_get.get('code'), dict) and post_create_get.get('code', {}).get('value') == new_location_code)
post_delete_error is not None and isinstance(post_delete_error, AlmaAPIError)

tests.scope: Confirm every input-validation guard added in issue #25 raises AlmaValidationError BEFORE any HTTP call is issued. Exercises Configuration._validate_code on the path-parameter side of every CRUD method (list_locations(''), get_location('', 'X'), get_location('LIB', ''), update_location('', 'X', {...}), update_location('LIB', '', {...}), delete_location('', 'X'), delete_location('LIB', '')) and Configuration._validate_location_data_for_create plus the update_location body guard on the payload side (create_location('LIB', None) / create_location('LIB', {}) / create_location with missing 'code', missing 'name', missing 'type', empty 'type' value; update_location with empty body). Pure runtime guard exercise; no SANDBOX state is read or mutated. Covers issue #25 AC 'Location creation validates required fields (code, name, type) before sending to API.' and the AlmaValidationError half of 'Errors raise AlmaValidationError (input) or AlmaAPIError / subclass (API).'

The input-validation half (AlmaValidationError on bad inputs) is mapped to t-25-2; the API-error half is partially mapped to t-25-1 (the post-delete get_location is asserted to raise AlmaAPIError, exercising the API-error propagation path on a deterministic 4xx). The remaining API-error subclass mapping (e.g. AlmaRateLimitError, AlmaAuthenticationError) is environment-dependent and cannot be provoked deterministically against SANDBOX; existing unit tests in tests/unit/domains/test_configuration.py cover these with mocked HTTP. This entry documents the residual unit-only facet for the typed-subclass cases.
